scenarios.title: Molecular ML, Drug Discovery, and Chemistry Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Atomistic machine-learning workflows involving molecules, materials, neural network potentials, quantum-chemical properties, ASE databases, force fields, molecular dynamics, or SchNetPack CLI/API usage.
scenarios.role: SchNetPack-specific guidance for atomistic neural network potentials, datasets, Hydra training/prediction configs, ASE/MD interfaces, and LAMMPS deployment.
scenarios.read_when: Use for requests naming SchNetPack, schnetpack, spktrain, spkpredict, spkconvert, spkdeploy, spkmd, SchNet, PaiNN, AtomisticModel, NeuralNetworkPotential, ASEAtomsData, AtomsDataModule, SpkCalculator, atomistic datasets, neural network potentials, energy/force/stress model training, QM9/MD17/rMD17 configs, ASE calculators, molecular dynamics, or LAMMPS pair_style schnetpack.
scenarios.best_for: Creating and validating ASE DB datasets, composing Hydra training or prediction commands, wiring atomistic model components and property keys, using trained models with ASE/MD, and preparing LAMMPS TorchScript deployment.
scenarios.avoid_when: Avoid for generic cheminformatics molecule manipulation without atomistic neural-network potentials, non-SchNetPack molecular simulation engines, or repository-maintenance tasks unrelated to SchNetPack workflows.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose schnetpack when the task involves SchNetPack-specific APIs, CLIs, Hydra configs, ASE DB metadata, atomistic neural network potential components, SchNet/PaiNN models, ASE calculators, MD configs, or LAMMPS deployment. Choose a more general chemistry or simulation skill when the package is not SchNetPack and the request is about another toolkit's molecule processing or trajectory engine.
